mission.title: Install and Configure Hami on Kubernetes
mission.description: HAMi is a Heterogeneous AI Computing Virtualization Middleware that allows Kubernetes users to manage various types of heterogeneous devices like GPUs and NPUs. Installing HAMi enables better scheduling and resource sharing among pods, making it essential for AI and machine learning workloads.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official HAMi Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add hami https://github.com/Project-HAMi/HAMi/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install HAMi using Helm
steps.description:
Install the HAMi chart in a new namespace called 'hami' with the following command:
```bash
helm install hami hami/hami --namespace hami --create-namespace --version 2.8.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the HAMi pods are running successfully with the following command:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace hami
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Edit the values.yaml file to set resource limits for the HAMi deployment. You can do this by running:
```bash
helm upgrade hami hami/hami --namespace hami --set resources.limits.cpu=1000m,resources.limits.memory=512Mi
```

steps.title: Set up RBAC for HAMi
steps.description:
Create a Role and RoleBinding to grant necessary permissions for HAMi:
```bash
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  namespace: hami
  name: hami-role
rules:
- apiGroups: [""] # core API group
  resources: ["pods", "pods/log"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
EOF

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: hami-role-binding
  namespace: hami
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: hami-role
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: default
  namespace: hami
EOF
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of HAMi will show the HAMi pods running in the 'hami' namespace. You can verify the deployment by checking the pod status and ensuring that the resource limits and RBAC configurations are properly set.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add hami https://github.com/Project-HAMi/HAMi/charts
helm repo update

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  namespace: hami
  name: hami-role
rules:
- apiGroups: [""] # core API group
  resources: ["pods", "pods/log"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
EOF

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: hami-role-binding
  namespace: hami
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: hami-role
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: default
  namespace: hami
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher and have Helm and kubectl installed.
